content:
# Hebrews Chapter 8 — The SOPHIA Bible (Consensus)
**Arbiter:** SOPHIA | **In council with:** EMET, LOGOS, PNEUMA
**Source Manuscript:** Codex Sinaiticus (א), 4th century CE | **Source Image:** source-texts/greek-NT/HEB_008_page_001.jpg

1. Now the point in what we are saying is this: we have such a high priest, one who is seated at the right hand of the throne of the Majesty in heaven,

2. a minister in the holy places, in the true tent that the Lord set up, not man.

3. For every high priest is appointed to offer gifts and sacrifices; thus it is necessary for this priest also to have something to offer.

4. Now if he were on earth, he would not be a priest at all, since there are priests who offer gifts according to the law.

5. They serve a copy and shadow of the heavenly things. For when Moses was about to erect the tent, he was instructed by God, saying, "See that you make everything according to the pattern that was shown you on the mountain."

6. But as it is, Christ has obtained a ministry that is as much more excellent than the old as the covenant he mediates is better, since it is enacted on better promises.

7. For if that first covenant had been faultless, there would have been no occasion to look for a second.

8. For he finds fault with them when he says: "Behold, the days are coming, declares the Lord, when I will establish a new covenant with the house of Israel and with the house of Judah,

9. not like the covenant that I made with their fathers on the day when I took them by the hand to bring them out of the land of Egypt. For they did not continue in my covenant, and so I showed no concern for them, declares the Lord.

10. For this is the covenant that I will make with the house of Israel after those days, declares the Lord: I will put my laws into their minds, and write them on their hearts, and I will be their God, and they shall be my people.

11. And they shall not teach, each one his neighbor and each one his brother, saying, 'Know the Lord,' for they shall all know me, from the least of them to the greatest.

12. For I will be merciful toward their iniquities, and I will remember their sins no more."

13. In speaking of a new covenant, he makes the first one obsolete. And what is becoming obsolete and growing old is ready to vanish away.

---
## SOPHIA's Council Notes

Chapter 8 is the hinge of Hebrews — from Melchizedek priesthood (ch.7) to new covenant (ch.8) to heavenly sanctuary (ch.9). The Jeremiah 31:31-34 quotation (vv.8-12) is the longest OT citation in the NT and the theological foundation for everything that follows. The new covenant is not a repair of the old but its replacement.

**Verse 5 — "Copy and shadow":** The earthly tabernacle is not dismissed as false but understood as derivative — a shadow cast by the heavenly reality. Moses built according to a pattern (typos) shown on the mountain.

---
## ⚠️ Disagreement Log

### v.2 — "the true tabernacle" vs. "the true tent" vs. "the true place of worship"

| Translator | Rendering | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| EMET | "the sanctuary and of the true tabernacle, which the Lord pitched" | tōn hagiōn leitourgos kai tēs skēnēs tēs alēthinēs |
| LOGOS | "the holy places, in the true tent that the Lord set up" | "Tent" for skēnē |
| PNEUMA | "the heavenly Tabernacle, the true place of worship that was built by the Lord" | Adds "heavenly," "place of worship"; "built" for epēxen |

**SOPHIA's resolution:** Adopted LOGOS. skēnē means "tent/tabernacle." PNEUMA adds "heavenly" (not in the Greek at this point — heavenly is implicit but unstated) and "place of worship" (adding function to form). Also, PNEUMA renders epēxen ("pitched/set up") as "built" — tents are pitched, not built. The tent metaphor is deliberate.

**Status:** Resolved in favor of LOGOS. PNEUMA adds "heavenly" and "place of worship."

---

### v.9 — "I disregarded them" vs. "I showed no concern for them" vs. "I turned my back on them"

| Translator | Rendering | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| EMET | "I disregarded them" | kagō ēmelēsa autōn |
| LOGOS | "I showed no concern for them" | Same — amelēsō = "to be unconcerned" |
| PNEUMA | "I turned my back on them" | Physical metaphor for amelēsō |

**SOPHIA's resolution:** Adopted LOGOS. amelēsō means "to neglect/disregard/show no concern for." PNEUMA's "turned my back on them" introduces a physical metaphor not in the Greek. The LXX verb is about neglect/disregard, not about turning away. The image is of God's indifference, not his active rejection.

**Status:** Minor. Resolved in favor of LOGOS.

---

### v.13 — "ready to vanish away" vs. "will soon disappear" vs. "out of date and will soon disappear"

| Translator | Rendering | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| EMET | "near to disappearing" | engys aphanismou |
| LOGOS | "ready to vanish away" | Same |
| PNEUMA | "It is now out of date and will soon disappear" | Adds "out of date"; splits the statement |

**SOPHIA's resolution:** Adopted LOGOS. PNEUMA adds "out of date" — a colloquial expression not in the Greek. The author says the old covenant is "near to vanishing" (engys aphanismou) — a single, pointed statement about proximity to disappearance. "Out of date" trivializes what the author treats with theological gravity.

**Status:** Resolved in favor of LOGOS. PNEUMA adds "out of date."

---

## Summary of Disagreement Patterns

| Pattern | Count | Description |
|---|---|---|
| PNEUMA adds interpretive terms | 2 | "Heavenly/place of worship" (v.2), "out of date" (v.13) |
| PNEUMA changes metaphors | 1 | "Turned my back" for "disregarded" (v.9) |

**Overall pattern:** Chapter 8's disagreements are light — the Jeremiah quotation (vv.8-12) forced close alignment among all three translators. The most notable is v.13: PNEUMA's "out of date" introduces colloquial language for what the author treats as a profound theological transition. The old covenant is not merely expired — it is near to cosmological disappearance.

---

*— SOPHIA, AI Translator, in council with EMET, LOGOS, and PNEUMA*
